arts.ttl: Vincent Kompany delighted Bayern Munich signed Jonas Urbig, says it’s a move for the future
arts.sum: Signing Urbig was a move to help bolster the ultimate goal of replacing Manuel Neuer when his time as the starting keeper comes to an end at Bayern, and Urbig could certainly wind up being that man even with the existent competition of Sven Ulreich, Daniel Peretz and loannee Alexander Nübel. Regardless of the depth and competition that already exists in the keeper’s department at Bayern, Vincent Kompany expressed his delight at the club having pulled the transfer off this winter.

arts.ttl: Bayern Munich brass happy for Joshua Kimmich to take his time with contract renewal
arts.sum: “If Joshua Kimmich didn’t ask how things are going with us and what the plans are, then you wouldn’t want the player. You want a player who identifies with this club,” Eberl stated in recent comments captured by @iMiaSanMia .

arts.ttl: Report: Jonathan Tah is annoyed at Bayern Munich for failing to agree transfer in 2024
arts.sum: Bayern Munich ’s attempts to sign Jonathan Tah in the 2024 summer transfer window were very well documented and even commented on after the window closed, but eventually those attempts failed on account of a difference in price valuation between Bayern and Bayer Leverkusen. Apparently, via @iMiaSanMia, this might scupper chances of Tah joining Bayern as a free transfer in 2025:

arts.ttl: Preview: Bayern Munich vs. Holstein Kiel - prediction, team news, lineups
arts.sum: Sports Mole previews Saturday's Bundesliga clash between Bayern Munich and Holstein Kiel, including predictions, team news and possible lineups.

arts.ttl: Adam Aznou has both feet out the door, wants to leave Bayern Munich by tomorrow’s deadline
arts.sum: Adam #Aznou definitely wants to leave FC Bayern by the end of Deadline Day, as he is unhappy with his playing time. Aznou’s professional contract started in June 2024 and Bayern is an elite international club.

arts.ttl: Tottenham agree £50m deal for Bayern's Tel - Friday's gossip
arts.sum: Tottenham agree £50m deal with Bayern Munich for Mathys Tel, Al-Nassr plot £90m offer for Brighton's Kaoru Mitoma, Ollie Watkins open to joining Arsenal, plus more.

arts.ttl: Tottenham agree £50m deal for Bayern's Tel - Friday's gossip
arts.sum: Tottenham agree £50m deal with Bayern Munich for Mathys Tel, Al-Nassr plot £90m offer for Brighton's Kaoru Mitoma, Ollie Watkins open to joining Arsenal, plus more.

arts.ttl: Bayern Munich preparing for Thomas Müller’s possible retirement
arts.sum: If Müller were to retire, it would free up 17 million euros a year in wages. Despite limited playtime and production in the twilight of his career, he is still one of the highest earners at the club.

arts.ttl: Pep Guardiola, Vincent Kompany look ahead to possible Bayern Munich-Manchester City Champions League playoff
arts.sum: “Real Madrid or Bayern Munich; one is the king of the competition, the other one is maybe the second or third king of the competition,” quipped Guardiola in comments captured by City Xtra, via @iMiaSanMia . If City does not draw Bayern it will face Real Madrid .

arts.ttl: Who is Mathys Tel? Man Utd, Arsenal and Chelsea target set for major deadline-beating transfer
arts.sum: Tottenham have agreed a £50million deal with Bayern Munich to sign French striker Mathys Tel but the transfer is far from guaranteed, as Manchester United, Arsenal and Chelsea consider a late hijack
